contraindications.clinicalRationale: FDA approval requires eGFR ≥30 ml/min per 1.73 m² for T2D and ASCVD glucose control, and eGFR ≥20 ml/min per 1.73 m² for heart failure indications. Clinical trials excluded patients below these thresholds.
contraindications.riskDescription: Reduced efficacy and potential safety concerns in severe kidney impairment
contraindications.alternativeConsiderations: Consider other diabetes medications appropriate for severe CKD stages
contraindications.sourceSnippet: FDA-approved eGFR ≥30 ml/min per 1.73 m² for T2D and ASCVD for glucose control, eGFR ≥20 ml/min per 1.73 m² for HF. Trial enrollment: EMPA-REG eGFR ≥30, EMPEROR-Reduced and EMPEROR-Preserved eGFR ≥20.

warnings.warningText: Monitor for genital mycotic infections and diabetic ketoacidosis (DKA) in patients treated with empagliflozin
warnings.actionRequired: Clinical monitoring for genital infections and DKA symptoms, especially in high-risk patients

warnings.warningText: Monitor kidney function as empagliflozin may cause an initial decline in eGFR that is typically reversible
warnings.actionRequired: Monitor eGFR regularly, especially during initiation. A drop in eGFR ≥10% does not necessarily require discontinuation based on post hoc analyses

warnings.warningText: Monitor for genital tract infections and urinary tract infections in heart failure patients
warnings.actionRequired: Clinical monitoring for genitourinary infections, particularly in patients receiving empagliflozin for heart failure indications

warnings.warningText: Monitor for hypotension in heart failure patients, particularly those with preserved ejection fraction
warnings.affectedPopulation: Heart failure patients with preserved ejection fraction receiving empagliflozin
warnings.actionRequired: Monitor blood pressure and assess for symptoms of hypotension, especially during treatment initiation
